title: Autonomous-PO runtime state
description: Schema for .scrum/autonomy.json. Tracks the autonomous-PO run loop: iteration counter, total spend, per-phase Stop-block budgets, circuit-breaker flag, last failure. Writers: hooks (hooks/lib/autonomy.sh helpers) and the watchdog process. Direct edits by agents are blocked by pre-tool-use-scrum-state-guard.sh.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for one autonomous-PO run (e.g. timestamp + nonce).

lead_session_id.description: Claude Code session ID that owns the lead loop. is_lead_session() compares against this. Null until the lead announces itself.

iteration.description: Current Stop-driven iteration count. Incremented by the Stop-loop hook.

total_cost_usd.description: Cumulative spend across all iterations in this run.

stop_blocks.description: Stop-block counter scoped to the current workflow phase. Resets when phase changes (see bump_stop_block_counter).

circuit_breaker_tripped.description: Set when stop-block budget is exhausted for a phase. While set, the lead session refuses to continue.

last_failure.description: Last observed unrecoverable failure (used by watchdog / dashboard).

watchdog_pid.description: PID of the watchdog process currently driving this run. Set at watchdog startup, cleared to null on clean exit. The Stop gate checks `kill -0 <pid>`: when the pid is absent/null or not alive, no outer loop will re-launch the session, so the gate falls back to human-mode Stop behaviour instead of block-every-Stop (which would storm a session nothing will resume).
